stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 15 website users. The sample mean is 40.5 ms with a sample standard deviation of 14.1 ms. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 39.2 ms and standard deviation of 8.7 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 40.5, 95% CI = [32.7, 48.3]. Without outliers: mean = 39.2, 95% CI = [34.4, 44.0]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [32.7, 48.3]. Point estimate: 39.9 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [32.7, 48.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
